title: Berlin U-Bahn Etiquette: Quick Summary
items: **Let people off before you get on.** Berliners wait at the sides of the doors, not in the middle. Crowding the doorway is the clearest tourist tell.
**Stand on the right of the escalator, walk on the left.** At deep stations like Alexanderplatz and Friedrichstraße the left side is a walking lane. **Rechts stehen, links gehen.**
**Many doors only open when you press the button.** Wait for it to light up, then push. The train does not open every door for you automatically.
**Leave the marked priority seats** free for elderly, disabled or pregnant passengers and people with small children, and take your backpack off in a crowded car.
**Keep it quiet.** Short calls, headphones for sound. A hot döner or strong-smelling food in a packed carriage is frowned on, even though there is no formal food ban.
**Alcohol is technically allowed** on the U-Bahn and S-Bahn but is not always welcome; read the carriage before you open a can.
**A bike needs its own ticket** and is not allowed during weekday rush hours (roughly 6–9am and 2–5pm); a fully folded bike rides free as luggage.
**Always carry a valid, validated ticket.** Open platforms have no barriers, but plain-clothes inspectors board without warning and riding without one costs a €60 fine.
